title: StressDream: Steering Video World Models for Robust Policy Evaluation and Improvement
summary: Video world models (WMs) can imagine realistic future observations conditioned on ego-robot actions, but policy evaluation and improvement typically rely on nominal imaginations that miss high-impact outcomes unless prohibitively many samples are drawn. StressDream is an inference-time method that steers WM imaginations toward high-impact yet plausible outcomes specified by text, by optimizing the initial noise of diffusion-based world models. It combines two objectives — a semantic objective using a Vision-Language Model that provides informative gradients by reasoning about the generated video, and a plausibility objective that prevents the optimized noise from drifting out-of-distribution. With state-of-the-art video world models for autonomous driving and robotic manipulation, StressDream steers imaginations toward outcomes such as task failures, enabling robust policy evaluation and improvement.
